mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS held near the zero lower bound (0.05%) compresses equity discount rates and pushes investors into risk assets, producing persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains near zero regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during zero-rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE spikes above 10% following a sharp shock, forward-looking equity markets price in an eventual labor market recovery ahead of realized data, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the unemployment shock is treated as a peak rather than a trend continuation.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 10% following a rise of more than 5pp within 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=14.7 as of 2020-04, up from ~4.4% in March
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes above 10% are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero in more than 40% of historical shock episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rebound in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) from depressed pandemic lows signals synchronized consumer and housing demand recovery, which is not captured by either series alone and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as reopening momentum broadens.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month increase > 5% concurrent with HOUST month-over-month increase > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=403946 and HOUST=1216 as of latest snapshot, both depressed from pre-COVID levels
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST rebound signals precede negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During an acute demand-shock recession regime, a continued month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals ongoing industrial capacity destruction and elevated near-term recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=92.5919 as of 2020-04, sharply down from pre-COVID levels near 110
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are positive or non-negative in more than 55% of months where INDPRO declines, or Rank IC is negative over any 1-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month decline in PAYEMS reflects deteriorating aggregate labor income and future consumption capacity, which mechanically depresses corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter, independent of any specific threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=131045 thousand as of 2020-04, down over 20 million from February 2020 peak
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no significant negative correlation with trailing 3-month PAYEMS change (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned at the zero lower bound while CPIAUCSL simultaneously decelerates or falls month-over-month, markets price rising deflation risk and diminished monetary policy traction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month until inflation stabilizes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.05% and CPIAUCSL=255.902 as of latest snapshot amid pandemic demand collapse
regime_hint: zero lower bound with deflation risk
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of months meeting the joint trigger condition, invalidating the deflation-risk repricing channel.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 registers a large quarterly contraction while RSAFS simultaneously shows a sharp month-over-month rebound, the divergence signals that high-frequency demand is recovering faster than backward-looking GDP data captures, leading markets to reward the surprise with positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized decline > 10% AND RSAFS month-over-month increase > 10%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=18987.877 as of 2020Q1 reflecting early pandemic contraction
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following this divergence pattern are negative or statistically indistinguishable from baseline in more than 50% of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime following a severe industrial production collapse, the first month-over-month uptick in INDPRO signals the trough of the supply-side contraction and is interpreted by markets as the start of a production recovery cycle, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month increase after two or more consecutive prior monthly declines exceeding 5% each
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=92.5919 as of 2020-04 following consecutive sharp declines from COVID shutdowns
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following the first INDPRO uptick after a collapse are negative or flat in more than 50% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
